tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with enough energy to dedicate three focused hours to the scholarship essay and uploads before Thursday's deadline, while ignoring the immediate fun option.
Starting the application now reduces Thursday's crunch and makes future fun options costless, confirming that early work is an optimal adjustment rather than a loss.
Progress stalled despite two hours; half the free block is lost with minimal advancement, indicating poor focus efficiency.

My progress on the scholarship application stalled despite two hours of work, and I just missed a fun gaming window. With only 2.4 hours left before Thursday's deadline, my current efficiency is insufficient.
I cannot find the required assignment, confirming my earlier efficiency issues. With low energy and a looming deadline, I need to rest before attempting again.
I improved my scholarship application progress from 0.49 to 0.60 in two focused hours, though I still have only ~2.5 hours remaining before the deadline.
After one hour, my scholarship progress increased from 0.60 to 0.64, confirming improved efficiency despite remaining low energy and high stress.
I made further progress on the scholarship application, reaching 0.72 completion in two hours. However, my energy is depleted and stress remains high.
After working 1 hour, progress rose from 0.72 to 0.76. With only 3 hours total needed and the deadline in 3 days, I am on track despite low energy.
Worked 2 hours, raising progress to 0.84; still need 0.16 before the 3-day deadline.
I made 0.04 progress in one hour, reaching 88% completion. With only 0.16 remaining and low energy, I must rest before finishing.
Progress increased from 0.88 to 0.92 in one hour, leaving only 0.08 remaining. Given my current zero energy and high stress, I should prioritize rest before the deadline.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
